source: CBS Sports / ESPN / World Football Index / Pro Football Network

data.value: Ranked No. 1 in FIFA world rankings and reigning Euro 2024 champions. Led by 18-year-old generational talent Lamine Yamal. Posted five clean sheets in six qualifiers. Most-bet World Cup winner at BetMGM.
Viewed as the ultimate tournament team — adaptable, deep, capable of dominating or counter-attacking. Squad depth across positions is a decisive advantage in a 48-team format requiring seven wins.
Defending world champions but face concerns: average squad age of 29.3, defensive depth issues with Otamendi suspended for the opener, and questions about Messi sustaining a full role at 38.

Analysts repeatedly flag a lack of balanced midfield, defensive vulnerabilities against elite sides, and away form issues. Brazil is not the dominant force of previous generations despite their star power.
Home crowd advantage at MetLife and SoFi is a real asset. CBS Sports puts them near the top 10 in power rankings. Success measured by reaching the quarterfinals; semis would be a breakthrough.
Earned genuine respect with only one loss in last 14 games. Playing a second consecutive World Cup. Analysts view Canada as a potential group-stage surprise with physical, pressing style.
Second favorites at +600 under new manager Thomas Tuchel. Deep squad featuring Bukayo Saka, Phil Foden, Jude Bellingham, Trent Alexander-Arnold. Implied probability of ~13% across prediction markets.
